description: OpenTimestamps receipt has one byte (offset 100 or the last byte if file shorter) XOR'd with 0x55, corrupting the proof past its magic header.

modifications.mutation: Byte at offset 100 (or length-1, whichever smaller) XOR'd with 0x55.
modifications.rationale: Documents V1 verifier behavior under OTS bytes tamper: check 2 catches the file SHA mismatch; check 5 does NOT (V1 doesn't cryptographically verify pending-state OTS receipts; pending-OTS warn is emitted regardless of byte-level mutations).

expected_failures: check 2 (artifact integrity): OTS receipt SHA-256 mismatch in manifest.
notes: V1 verifier does NOT fail check 5 on this fixture. The OTS receipt is in pending-Bitcoin-confirmation state; V1 emits a pending warn regardless of mutated bytes. A future verifier that runs the OTS protocol's calendar-attestation cross-check on pending receipts WOULD detect this tamper. The fixture is preserved as-is so a Phase 5+ tightening can update the expected status to fail.
